identity.canonical_title: Spectral Topology: KK Modes and Extra Dimensions: f_rm Obs

theory.canonical_statement: \section{Spectral Topology: KK Modes and Extra Dimensions} The observed spectrum may include contributions from compact extra dimensions. For a frequency $f_{\rm obs}$, suppose it relates to a base mode $f_0$ by \[ f_{\rm obs} = f_0 \sqrt{1 + \bigl(m \tfrac{c}{2\pi R_{\rm KK}}\bigr)^2}, \] where $m\in\mathbb{Z}$. We invert by scanning a tower of KK frequencies: \begin{verbatim} ReverseKaluzaKleinDecomposition[f_obs_, f0_, λ_ac_, λ_KK_] := Module[{m, spectrum, nearest, residual}, spectrum = Table[{n, f0 * Sqrt[1 + n^2*(λ_ac/λ_KK)^2]}, {n,0,100}]; nearest = Nearest[spectrum[[All,2]]->spectrum[[All,1]], f_obs][[1]]; m = nearest; residual = f_obs - (m*c)/(2*Pi*λ_KK); Return[{m, residual}]; ] \end{verbatim} This yields a KK index $m$ and a residual offset.
theory.technical_interpretation: Spectral Topology: KK Modes and Extra Dimensions: f_rm Obs is an algebraic definition, written f_rm obs = f_0 sqrt1 + l(m tfracc2π R_rm KK r)^2. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from π, f_0, m, c, R_ KK, defining f_rm obs. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.inputs: π, f_0, m, c, R_ KK
